scores.materiality_reasoning: Protection from execution is a core bodily entitlement and physical protection.
scores.generality_reasoning: Right to life/bodily integrity is a foundational societal axis.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Protection from capital punishment is a lifelong structural status for citizens.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Citizenship is a legal birth-status, very hard to change or acquire.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Andocides describes contemporary Athenian legal norms during his trial.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reported as an established expectation, though framed for persuasion.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a citizen does not inherently define one as immune to execution.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Exclusion from the Agora has direct legal and economic material effects.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to a specific location during a specific legal procedure.
scores.persistence_reasoning: The exclusion is bounded to the duration of the murder trial.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Group is defined here as individuals 'charged with murder', a legal role.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Antiphon describes the standing law governing Athenian murder trials.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States the law 'debarred by proclamation' as an absolute factual rule.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being charged with murder does not definitionally include Agora exclusion.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Voluntary exile is a substitute for death, impacting physical freedom/life.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific right recurring in the context of capital trials.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Bounded to a single procedural moment during a trial.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Group is 'defendants in murder trials', an episodic legal role.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes a well-documented Athenian legal privilege of the time.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States the 'right given to every one' as a matter of fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Defendant status does not definitionally imply the right to flee.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Avoiding pretrial imprisonment is a direct protection of physical freedom.
scores.generality_reasoning: Broad legal protection from detention spanning many trial types.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Bounded to the period between indictment and verdict.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Metic/Alien status is a durable legal identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Cites a specific contemporary law ('the law concerned applies').
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Asserts the law and universal practice ('no alien... has ever been').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being an alien does not definitionally require providing sureties.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Access to sacred buildings is primarily symbolic/ceremonial.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to trial locations and temple access.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Bounded to the duration of the state of ritual pollution.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Defilement is an episodic/event-based condition.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to contemporary religious and judicial customs.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Frames it as common knowledge ('as of course you all know').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Impurtiy/uncleanliness definitionally entails exclusion from sacred or 'pure' spaces.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Authority over capital punishment is an ultimate material power.
scores.generality_reasoning: Broad jurisdictional right governing life and death.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Structural restriction on a state's legal capacity while in alliance.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Allied state membership is a durable political identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes the 5th-century imperial relationship between Athens and allies.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States the restriction as a clear legal prohibition.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being an ally does not definitionally forbid independent executions.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Right to trial vs summary execution is a matter of life/death.
scores.generality_reasoning: Recurring procedural right for a specific class of crime.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Applies during the procedural window following a crime.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slave status is a legal birth-status/structural identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Cites the 'ancient laws of your country' in current use.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Asserts what the law ordains as factual practice.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Slave status does not definitionally include the right to a trial.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Judicial torture is a profound violation of bodily integrity.
scores.generality_reasoning: Standard legal procedure affecting all slave testimony.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Specific to the duration of the interrogation.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Slave status is a structural/legal identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Describes standard 5th-century Athenian legal practice.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Refers to the act of examination under torture as standard procedure.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a slave does not definitionally imply being tortured in court.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Retention of a specific name is a symbolic/trait-based resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Hyper-specific resource: the linguistic label of an individual.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Names usually last for a life-stage or employment duration.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Servant/slave is a structural birth-status/legal identity.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Plato cites this as a common contemporary social habit.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Uses the real practice as an undisputed premise for argument.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Servitude does not definitionally involve name-changing by a master.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The power to legislate/name is a social role with legal impact.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the domain of linguistic and legal naming.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Established names have durable structural force in society.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Unskilled/common status is a wealth/education bracket.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discusses contemporary theories of naming and law-making.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Verbatim is a philosophical argument about who 'ought' to name.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Lack of skill does not definitionally exclude one from naming.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Wisdom and its recognition are non-material/trait-based.
scores.generality_reasoning: Wisdom is seen as a general attribute affecting many life roles.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Gendered traits were viewed as lifelong and unchanging.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Biological sex is strictly immutable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reflects the common social consensus of the author's time.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports a general consensus ('do you not think... the men?').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Gender is not defined by wisdom levels.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Hereditary transmission of status has major material consequences.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers a broad class of political and social standing.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Hereditary title and class are lifelong and structural.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Lineage and being the 'son of a king' are birth-fixed.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Discusses kingship as a known, albeit often foreign or heroic, model.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Woven into an argument about names, but reflects social reality.
scores.tautology_reasoning: The 'son of a king' definitionally acquires the status of a king.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Supervision of law-making is a high-level social/political role.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the domain of legislation and naming.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Durable professional authority of the dialectician role.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Dialectician is an identity acquired through training.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Pertains to philosophical roles in Plato's intellectual circle.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Purely normative/ideal proposal by Socrates.
scores.tautology_reasoning: A dialectician is not definitionally a legislative supervisor.

scores.materiality_reasoning: The right to address gods in prayer is a purely spiritual resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Broad religious practice covering all acts of prayer.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Lifelong access to traditional religious forms.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Humanity ('Men') is biologically immutable.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to 'customary' practice in contemporary prayers.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports a social fact ('as is customary').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being human does not definitionally include specific prayer rights.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Right to bear a specific name affects legal personhood and inheritance.
scores.generality_reasoning: Recurring convention in family and civic life.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Ancestral names are typical lifelong identifiers.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Lineage/Ancestry is birth-fixed.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reflects standard Athenian family naming conventions.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports it as a common social convention ('often given because').
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being a descendant does not definitionally require taking a name.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Participation in purification rituals is a symbolic/ceremonial resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to recovery from 'possession' or intellectual intensity.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Momentary event to cleanse a specific state.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Defined by a transient state: post-intellectual inspiration.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to actual contemporary specialists (priests/sophists).
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports a real duty/practice expected in such circumstances.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Inspiration does not definitionally require ritual purification.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Ritual precedence is a ceremonial/religious honour.
scores.generality_reasoning: Narrowly scoped to a specific ordering of a specific ritual.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Recurring structural feature of annually held sacrifices.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Defined by the act of performing a sacrifice.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Refers to ‘those who established’ sacrifices in living memory/myth.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: States the sacrificial order as an established religious fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Sacrificing does not definitionally require Hestia to go first.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Divine association in the afterlife is purely spiritual.
scores.generality_reasoning: Covers the entire post-mortem state of the soul.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Hades' society is permanent/eternal in this framework.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Soul purity in this context is treated as a moral/metaphysical state.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Part ofcontemporary Platonic/Orphic theological belief.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Theological argument/interpretation of a deity's nature.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Purity of soul does not definitionally include Hades' society.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Association with a goddess is a symbolic/religious resource.
scores.generality_reasoning: Specific to the domain of Artemis’s patronage.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Holds as long as the life-stage of virginity lasts.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Virginity is a physical state (though leaveable, it is birth-fixed).
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Reflects active contemporary Greek mythological structures.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Mixes etymological theory with reported mythological attribute.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Virginity does not definitionally imply association with Artemis.

scores.materiality_reasoning: Linguistic autonomy affects education and cultural production.
scores.generality_reasoning: Spans the use of entire categories of vocabulary.
scores.persistence_reasoning: Loanwords persist across generations within the language.
scores.immutability_reasoning: Ethnicity (Greek) is fixed by birth/culture.
scores.contemporarity_reasoning: Observes current linguistic realities of the author's world.
scores.opinion_vs_fact_reasoning: Reports linguistic borrowing as a plain observable fact.
scores.tautology_reasoning: Being Greek does not definitionally forbid foreign word derivation.
